mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate exceeding a 0.5 percentage point monthly increase signals an abrupt labor market shock. This triggers risk-off repricing as investors anticipate falling corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5pp in a single month
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 60% of triggered instances across a 5-year out-of-sample test, or the cross-sectional predictive power drops below 0.02 Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth accelerates while consumer credit outstanding stagnates, it indicates a supply-driven expansion decoupled from demand. This mismatch typically precedes inventory corrections and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no statistically significant predictive power (p > 0.10) for 20-day returns in a rolling 3-year window, or the signal direction reverses during demand-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: During periods where the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5 percent, inflation expectations embedded in price data drive real yield compression. Rising CPIAUCSL in this high-rate environment signals accommodative real policy, which historically supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns turn negative in over 55% of observations where CPIAUCSL rises while FEDFUNDS remains above 2.5%, invalidating the real yield compression channel.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate of 2.0 percent confirms robust aggregate demand expansion. This fundamental strength reduces equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the immediate 5 trading day window.
failure_condition: The quarterly GDP growth signal produces negative or flat forward returns in more than 50% of instances, or the predictive Rank IC falls below 0.01 over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the industrial production index below 98.0 indicates a supply-side bottleneck or contraction. This physical output shortfall forces firms to raise prices or cut guidance, triggering earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold breach fails to predict negative returns in more than 65% of cases across a 5-year walk-forward test, or the signal generates positive returns during supply shocks due to pricing power effects.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls growth exceeds 200,000 per month while the unemployment rate simultaneously falls below 4.0 percent, it signals a wage-price spiral risk. This dual tightening forces the Federal Reserve to accelerate policy normalization, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS and UNRATE signal shows no significant predictive power (p > 0.10) for 60-day returns in a rolling 3-year window, or the mechanism reverses direction when inflation is below 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing market expansion regime, monthly housing starts exceeding 1.5 million units signal strong consumer confidence and durable goods demand. This leading indicator typically precedes broad economic acceleration, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts above 1.5 million units fail to predict positive returns in more than 55% of instances over a 10-year sample, or the signal loses predictive power when mortgage rates exceed 6.0 percent.
